source_paper_title: Indefinite Linear-Quadratic Partially Observed Mean-Field Game
context:
Page 14, Section 4.3, Remark 4.13(ii)

"Noticing that Riccati equation (35) is an asymmetric indefinite equation with complex structure, its solvability is still an problem."
title: Solvability of the asymmetric indefinite Riccati equation for the mean-field consistency condition
latex:
Given a finite horizon \([0,T]\) and dimensions \(n,m\in\mathbb{N}\), let \(A(\cdot),\bar A(\cdot),D(\cdot),\bar D(\cdot)\in L^{\infty}([0,T];\mathbb{R}^{n\times n})\), \(B(\cdot),\bar B(\cdot),F(\cdot),\bar F(\cdot)\in L^{\infty}([0,T];\mathbb{R}^{n\times m})\), and \(Q(\cdot)\in L^{\infty}([0,T];\mathbb{S}^n)\), \(R(\cdot)\in L^{\infty}([0,T];\mathbb{S}^m)\), \(S(\cdot)\in L^{\infty}([0,T];\mathbb{R}^{n\times m})\) be deterministic matrix-valued coefficients (not assumed positive semidefinite), and let \(\alpha_1,\alpha_2,\alpha_3,\beta_1,\beta_2\in\mathbb{R}\) be constants. For a matrix-valued function \(\Sigma:[0,T]\to\mathbb{R}^{n\times n}\), define
\[
R_e(t)= (1-\beta_1)R(t) + F(t)^\top \Sigma(t)\bigl(F(t)+\bar F(t)\bigr),
\]
\[
\Sigma_e(t)= \Sigma(t)\bigl(B(t)+\bar B(t)\bigr) + D(t)^\top\Sigma(t)\bigl(F(t)+\bar F(t)\bigr) + (1-\beta_2)S(t),
\]
\[
\bar\Sigma(t)= \Sigma(t)\bar B(t) + (D(t)+\bar D(t))^\top\Sigma(t)F(t) + (1-\alpha_2)S(t).
\]
Consider the (generally non-symmetric and indefinite) matrix Riccati differential equation
\[
\dot\Sigma(t) + \Sigma(t)\bigl(A(t)+\bar A(t)\bigr) + A(t)^\top\Sigma(t) + D(t)^\top\Sigma(t)\bigl(D(t)+\bar D(t)\bigr)
- \Sigma_e(t)\,R_e(t)^{-1}\,\bar\Sigma(t)^\top + (1-\alpha_1)Q(t) = 0,
\]
with terminal condition
\[
\Sigma(T) = (1-\alpha_3)L_T,
\]
where \(L_T\in\mathbb{S}^n\) is given.

The problem is to give verifiable conditions on the coefficients under which there exists a (necessarily unique, if it exists) solution \(\Sigma\in C([0,T];\mathbb{R}^{n\times n})\) such that \(R_e(t)\) is invertible for all \(t\in[0,T]\) (e.g., \(R_e(t)+R_e(t)^\top\gg 0\) or another suitable coercivity condition), i.e., to characterize well-posedness/solvability of this asymmetric indefinite Riccati equation (and thereby the mean-field consistency condition feedback law) in the general setting.
open_problem_quote: "Noticing that Riccati equation (35) is an asymmetric indefinite equation with complex structure, its solvability is still an problem."

verification.notice: This entry was generated from the paper text only. It has not been independently verified as still open. If you believe the question is solved or incorrectly stated, please contact the maintainers.
